description: Engine-specific GraphQL attacks beyond the generic introspection/depth/batch covered by graphql.json. Hasura (x-hasura-admin-secret bypass, admin role escalation), Apollo (APQ poisoning, federation introspection), Dgraph (mutation auth), PostGraphile (RLS bypass via _ prefix), Strawberry (Python-specific). Auto-probe enabled — engine-fingerprint matchers gate execution.

hasura_admin_secret_check.description: Hasura GraphQL Engine — admin secret enforcement check. If x-hasura-admin-secret absent on /v1/graphql or /v1/metadata and introspection still works, no admin secret configured = admin-level access for everyone.

probes.description: Introspection without admin-secret header. If server returns full schema, no auth required.
Hasura /v1/metadata export_metadata — anonymous call reveals entire backend config (db connections, remote schemas, action handlers, cron triggers)

hasura_role_header_escalation.description: Hasura uses x-hasura-role header. If JWT claims_map / unauthorized_role mis-set, attacker sends `x-hasura-role: admin` and gets admin-level query results.

probes.description: Header injection — bypass role enforcement if JWT verification doesn't pin role

hasura_remote_schema_ssrf.description: Hasura's remote_schema feature lets admin register an upstream GraphQL endpoint. If `add_remote_schema` reachable by attacker (because admin-secret absent / leaked), point at internal AWS metadata or attacker server → SSRF + arbitrary backend data inclusion.

probes.description: Register remote schema pointing at AWS IMDS. Subsequent introspect call returns metadata response.

apollo_persisted_query_poisoning.description: Apollo APQ (Automatic Persisted Queries) — client sends sha256Hash of query; if server caches hash→query mapping without auth check, attacker POSTs the unsigned hash + a crafted full query body and pollutes the cache.

probes.description: First call: with hash but no query → server returns PersistedQueryNotFound. Second call: include both — if accepted, hash now permanent. Subsequent calls with that hash from any caller return cached crafted query.

apollo_federation_internals.description: Apollo Federation — _service / _entities special root fields expose subgraph SDL. Often Apollo Gateway sits in front of internal services; querying _service against the gateway leaks internal subgraph names + routes.

probes.description: Federation reflection — returns full SDL of subgraph
Federation _entities resolver — accepts attacker-controlled representations. Some gateways trust __typename and skip authz on the resulting fetch.

dgraph_admin_unauth.description: Dgraph — /admin endpoint exposes shutdown / draining / schema / backup operations. Auth via x-dgraph-accesstoken or x-dgraph-authtoken; default install has neither.

postgraphile_underscore_bypass.description: PostGraphile auto-generates schema from Postgres tables. Fields starting with `_` are stripped by default — but if app code does its own RLS via session.user_id, an attacker can sometimes pass session variables via x-pg-* headers when PostGraphile's pgSettings plugin isn't locked.

probes.description: Inject pgSettings via header. If pgDefaultRole / pgSettings plugin reads from request, RLS variables forged.
Enumerate types — exposes underlying Postgres tables 1:1

graphql_field_suggestion_oracle.description: Most engines (graphql-js, apollo-server, ariadne) suggest similar field names when query fails — usable as oracle to brute-force schema even when introspection disabled. Single-character mutations reveal close field names.

probes.description: Misspelled field — server returns 'Did you mean password?' suggestion

graphql_alias_amplification_dos.description: Boundary case for DoS — aliased same-field repetition multiplies cost N times in single request. Don't run massive amplifications (Rule 5 DoS) but a single 50-alias query proves the absence of an alias limit.

probes.description: Minimal alias test — 5 aliases of same query. If response includes all 5 entries, no alias dedup; document and stop.

strawberry_python_specifics.description: Strawberry GraphQL (Python) — debug mode + dev-tools enabled in non-prod environments leaked to prod. /graphql/schema endpoint may expose raw SDL.
